files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.BridgeAudioFormat
files.Summary: Audio format delivered to and expected from a media consumer (the "bridge"/tap), such as an AI realtime bridge. When it differs from the negotiated wire codec, the SDK transcodes audio transparently so the consumer sees a single, fixed codec regardless of what the SIP peer negotiated.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ConnectOptions
files.Summary: Options for convenience registration flows.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ConnectResult
files.Summary: Result model for convenience registration/connect operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ConnectStatus
files.Summary: Status for convenience registration/connect operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Calls.CallManager
files.Summary: Registry of the SDK's live calls. Exposes the active-call collection, lookup, and add/remove/state-change notifications. Instances are created by the SDK, not by consumers.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Calls.ICallManager
files.Summary: Registry of the SDK's live calls. Exposes the active-call collection, lookup, and add/remove/state-change notifications.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Lines.IPhoneLineManager
files.Summary: Registry of the SDK's phone lines. Registers new SIP accounts, unregisters lines, exposes the current line collection, and aggregates each line's inbound-call notifications.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Lines.PhoneLineManager
files.Summary: Registry of the SDK's phone lines. Registers new SIP accounts, unregisters lines, exposes the current line collection, and aggregates each line's inbound-call notifications. Instances are created by the SDK, not by consumers.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.AudioFileFormat
files.Summary: Audio file container format used by recording and playback sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IMediaManager
files.Summary: Factory and orchestration entrypoint for media routing, recording and playback.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IMediaReceiver
files.Summary: Public per-call tap for inbound media frames. Multiple receivers can be attached to the same call in parallel (e.g. default audio, recording and a realtime bridge); every attached receiver observes every inbound frame.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IMediaSender
files.Summary: Public per-call injection point for outbound media frames. Frames are handed to the call's active send path; the payload must already be encoded in the negotiated codec (see for payload type and clock rate).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IMixedMediaBus
files.Summary: Abstraction for a mixed PCM16 media bus that supports recording taps and playback injection.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IPlaybackSession
files.Summary: Public handle for one active playback session.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IRecordingEncryptionProvider
files.Summary: Optional provider that encrypts finalized recording files.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IRecordingSession
files.Summary: Public handle for one active recording session.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IVideoReceiver
files.Summary: Public per-call tap for inbound encoded video frames. Multiple receivers can attach to the same call in parallel; every attached receiver observes every reassembled inbound frame. The SDK is transport-only — decode the payload with your own codec.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.IVideoSender
files.Summary: Public per-call injection point for outbound encoded video frames. Frames are handed to the call's active video send path; the payload must already be encoded in the negotiated codec (see for the negotiated media). The SDK is transport-only and never encodes.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaConnector
files.Summary: Connects media receivers to media senders with bounded in-memory backpressure.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaFrame
files.Summary: One encoded audio frame on the public media contract. The payload carries codec data as negotiated for the call (identified by PayloadType, e.g. 0 = PCMU, 8 = PCMA, 9 = G.722); it is not decoded PCM.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaFrameReceivedEventArgs
files.Summary: Event payload for . Delivered synchronously on the media path — handlers must return quickly (see the event's documentation).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaManager
files.Summary: Factory and orchestration entrypoint for media routing, recording and playback.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaReceiver
files.Summary: Surfaces a call's inbound (decoded) audio frames to the application via the event. Attach to a call to start receiving; a faulting subscriber is isolated so it neither stops other subscribers nor the RTP callback.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaSender
files.Summary: Feeds application-supplied audio frames into a call's outbound RTP stream. Attach to a call, then push frames with . Frames sent while the call is not connected/on-hold are silently dropped rather than throwing.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaSessionErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Error payload raised by recording and playback sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaSessionState
files.Summary: Runtime state for recording/playback media sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.MediaSessionStateChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Describes one media session state transition.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.PlaybackOptions
files.Summary: Runtime options for playback sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.PlaybackRequest
files.Summary: Input payload for starting a playback session.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.RecordingOptions
files.Summary: Configuration for call/conference recording sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.VideoBitrateRecommendationEventArgs
files.Summary: Event payload for : the SDK's ready-to-use outbound video bitrate recommendation and the coarse network quality behind it. Set your encoder's target bitrate to — the SDK never encodes (transport-only).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.VideoFrame
files.Summary: One encoded video frame on the public media contract. The SDK is transport-only: the payload carries already-encoded codec bytes (one coded picture) as negotiated for the call — it is not decoded pixels, and the SDK never encodes or decodes. Bring your own codec (VP8, H.264, …) and read to identify it.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.VideoFrameReceivedEventArgs
files.Summary: Event payload for . Delivered synchronously on the media path — handlers must return quickly (see the event's documentation).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.VideoReceiver
files.Summary: Surfaces a call's inbound encoded video frames to the application via the event. Attach to a call to start receiving; a faulting subscriber is isolated so it neither stops other subscribers nor the RTP callback. Mirrors for video — transport-only, so the payload is encoded, not decoded.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media.VideoSender
files.Summary: Feeds application-supplied encoded video frames into a call's outbound RTP stream. Attach to a call, then push frames with . Frames sent while the call is not connected/on-hold are silently dropped rather than throwing. Mirrors for video — transport-only, so the payload must already be encoded.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallActionResult
files.Summary: Unified result model for extended call control operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallActionStatus
files.Summary: Outcome category for extended call control operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallDirection
files.Summary: Indicates whether a call was initiated by the local party or received from a remote party.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallIceCandidate
files.Summary: Represents one ICE candidate associated with a call media leg.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallIceSnapshot
files.Summary: Read-only snapshot of ICE (RFC 8445 / RFC 7675) connectivity establishment for one call media leg. Produced once candidate-pair selection completes and exposed via . It explains which local/remote candidate pair media flows over and why — otherwise an ICE-driven media path or teardown is opaque to the consumer. Non-ICE calls never produce a snapshot ( stays null).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallIceState
files.Summary: Public lifecycle state of ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity establishment for one call media leg, surfaced read-only via . Non-ICE calls never produce an ICE snapshot, so this state is only meaningful when ICE was negotiated for the leg.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallId
files.Summary: Strongly-typed, immutable identifier for a single call instance.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallMediaParameters
files.Summary: Negotiated media parameters for one active call leg. Produced by the infrastructure adapter after SDP offer/answer exchange and consumed by the application media orchestrator to set up RTP I/O. A record so a derived leg (e.g. after ICE candidate-pair selection) can be produced with a with expression that carries every field across — a hand-written copy had silently dropped the SDES/DTLS key material and video parameters when overriding the endpoints.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallQualitySnapshot
files.Summary: Public quality snapshot for one call media leg. Values are updated continuously while the call is active and can be used by SDK consumers for routing, alerting, or UI quality indicators.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallRtpStatistics
files.Summary: Raw RTP transport statistics for one active call media leg, exposed for diagnostics, billing and troubleshooting. Unlike — which surfaces derived quality metrics (jitter/loss in ms/%, MOS) — this record carries the underlying RFC 3550 counters (SSRC identifiers, packet/octet counts, RTP-unit jitter) without interpretation. Available on once media is flowing.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallState
files.Summary: Lifecycle state of a call.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.CallVideoParameters
files.Summary: Negotiated video parameters for one call leg (WebRTC phase 2). Present on only when the SDP exchange negotiated an active video m-line with a codec the SDK supports; null keeps the call audio-only. Grouped into one object so parameter enrichment passes video through as a unit.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.DialOptions
files.Summary: Per-call dialing options — a pure Domain value object supplied to .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.DtmfTone
files.Summary: Validated DTMF tone (0-9, *, #, A-D).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.ICall
files.Summary: A single call and its lifecycle.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.NetworkQuality
files.Summary: Coarse, ready-to-use indicator of the media path's health, derived by the SDK from the congestion signal (delay trend + loss). Meant for a simple UI hint or a plugin decision — read it instead of interpreting raw metrics yourself. A call-media value describing the leg, alongside .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.CallActivityEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for call-registry activity events (a call being added or removed).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.CallErrorEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for a call-related error notification.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.CallIceConnectionStateChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the call IceConnectionStateChanged event: a running change of the ICE transport state after establishment (for example → on RFC 7675 consent loss). Complements the one-shot , which only reports the final establishment state.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.CallQualitySnapshotChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Event arguments for call quality snapshot updates.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.CallStateChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the call StateChanged event.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.DtmfReceivedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the call DtmfReceived event.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.HoldStateChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the call HoldStateChanged event.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.IncomingCallEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the line IncomingCall event.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.LineReconnectFailedEventArgs
files.Summary: Raised when a phone line has permanently failed to re-register and entered . No further reconnect attempts will be made.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.LineReconnectingEventArgs
files.Summary: Raised when a phone line begins a reconnect attempt after its SIP registration was lost. The line is already in when this event fires.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.LineStateChangedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the line StateChanged event.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Events.TransferRequestedEventArgs
files.Summary: Payload for the call TransferRequested event (an inbound SIP REFER).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.IPhoneLine
files.Summary: A registered SIP line (account) that places and receives calls.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.LineId
files.Summary: Stable, opaque identifier for a phone line.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.LineState
files.Summary: Describes the current registration state of a phone line.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.ReregisterFailReason
files.Summary: Describes why a phone line permanently failed to re-register and entered .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.ReregisterOptions
files.Summary: Controls automatic re-registration behavior when a SIP registration is lost due to a network disruption or server restart.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.SipAccount
files.Summary: Configuration of a SIP account used to register a and place/receive calls.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.SipAddress
files.Summary: Immutable, validated SIP address (e.g. sip:alice.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.SipCredentials
files.Summary: Immutable SIP authentication credentials.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.SipTransport
files.Summary: Supported SIP signaling transports on line-account level.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Security.SrtpDecisionReasonCodes
files.Summary: Stable reason-code constants for SRTP policy and negotiation decisions. These values are emitted in call media metadata and SIP telemetry.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Security.SrtpPolicy
files.Summary: Controls whether media (RTP) is protected with SRTP for a call.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.CalloraBuilder
files.Summary: Builder for optional CalloraVoipSdk dependency overrides.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.CalloraHostedService
files.Summary: Coordinates CalloraVoipSdk lifecycle through host start/stop hooks.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.CalloraStunServerBuilder
files.Summary: Builder for optional STUN server-host overrides (Level 3). Returned by .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.CalloraTurnServerBuilder
files.Summary: Builder for optional TURN/STUN server-host overrides (Level 3). Returned by ; mirrors CalloraWebRtcBuilder.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.CalloraWebRtcBuilder
files.Summary: Builder for optional WebRTC-facade dependency overrides (Level 3). Mirrors for the SIP facade and is returned by and by .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.ServiceCollectionExtensions
files.Summary: Dependency-injection entrypoint for CalloraVoipSdk.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.StunServerServiceCollectionExtensions
files.Summary: Dependency-injection entrypoint for the STUN server host. Standalone counterpart to for a pure-STUN (Binding) deployment.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.TurnServerServiceCollectionExtensions
files.Summary: Dependency-injection entrypoint for the TURN/STUN server host. Standalone counterpart to AddCalloraVoip / AddCalloraWebRtc: a host that runs a TURN server calls and receives a builder for optional overrides.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.VoipOptionsValidator
files.Summary: Startup validation for host-bound .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection.WebRtcServiceCollectionExtensions
files.Summary: Dependency-injection entrypoint for the WebRTC facade. Standalone counterpart to : a pure-WebRTC host calls only ; a host that wants both facades chains AddCalloraVoip(...).AddWebRtc(...) (ADR-012, two-facade composition).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DeviceManager
files.Summary: Runtime audio device control facade.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DialResult
files.Summary: Result model for convenience outbound dial-and-wait operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DialStatus
files.Summary: Status for convenience outbound dial-and-wait operations.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.DialWaitOptions
files.Summary: Options for convenience outbound dialing flows.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.IStunServerHost
files.Summary: A hostable STUN server (RFC 5389) for server-reflexive address discovery — the pure-STUN counterpart to . It binds its socket on construction (so is known immediately, including after an ephemeral bind), answers Binding requests from , and releases everything on disposal.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.ITurnServerHost
files.Summary: A hostable TURN relay server (RFC 8656) — the server-side counterpart to the client facades (VoipClient, WebRtcClient). It binds its socket on construction (so is known immediately, including after an ephemeral bind), starts serving on , and stops and releases everything on disposal. It serves the TURN methods (Allocate / Refresh / CreatePermission / ChannelBind / Send / Data, plus the TCP extension); it does not answer bare STUN Binding requests — for server-reflexive discovery run an alongside it.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.StunServerHost
files.Summary: The public STUN server host (Level 2 facade). Assembles the internal from a — binding the socket on construction — and answers Binding requests with a once started. Kept in the Client layer so the server infrastructure stays an internal implementation detail reached only through this facade.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.StunServerHostConfiguration
files.Summary: Immutable configuration a is built from (Level 2). A STUN Binding server needs no credentials; it just returns the client's server-reflexive (mapped) address.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.StunServerHostOptions
files.Summary: Mutable options bound from the DI container (Level 3), mapped onto the immutable when the host is resolved.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.TurnServerCredential
files.Summary: One long-term credential the hosted TURN server accepts (RFC 8656 / RFC 5389 §10.2). The realm is set once on the host configuration (); each credential carries only the username and password.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.TurnServerHost
files.Summary: The public TURN/STUN server host (Level 2 facade). Assembles the internal from a — binding the socket on construction — and exposes only the small hostable surface (). Kept in the Client layer so the server infrastructure stays an internal implementation detail, reached only through this facade.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.TurnServerHostConfiguration
files.Summary: Immutable configuration a is built from (Level 2). Mirrors the client facades' configuration objects (VoipConfiguration, WebRtcConfiguration): the mutable maps onto this for the DI happy path.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.TurnServerHostOptions
files.Summary: Mutable options bound from the DI container (Level 3), mapped onto the immutable when the host is resolved. Mirrors WebRtcOptions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IDeviceManager
files.Summary: Runtime audio device control facade.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IModuleManager
files.Summary: Runtime availability facade for optional SDK modules.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IModuleRegistry
files.Summary: Thread-safe registry resolving optional SDK modules by their feature contract.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IPolicyManager
files.Summary: Policy facade exposing active SDK policy defaults.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IQualityManager
files.Summary: Runtime quality facade for call-level snapshots and subscriptions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ISessionManager
files.Summary: Read-only runtime view over active calls and media sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ITelemetryManager
files.Summary: Telemetry facade for SIP events, metrics, and call-detail records.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IVoipClient
files.Summary: Public SDK client contract for dependency-injection and consumer testability.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IceConfiguration
files.Summary: Controls ICE gathering and connectivity checks for call media sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IceOptions
files.Summary: Mutable ICE options model for DI-based SDK configuration.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IceServerConfiguration
files.Summary: Defines one ICE helper server entry used by the call media runtime.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IceServerType
files.Summary: Classifies one ICE helper server entry.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.IceTransport
files.Summary: Transport protocol used to connect to STUN/TURN servers.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ModuleManager
files.Summary: Runtime availability facade for optional SDK modules.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.ModuleRegistry
files.Summary: Thread-safe registry resolving optional SDK modules by their feature contract. Modules are contributed by separate packages either through dependency injection (register services before AddCalloraVoip) or programmatically via .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Modules.IPlaybackModule
files.Summary: SDK facade for playback features.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Modules.IRecordingModule
files.Summary: SDK facade for recording features.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Modules.IVoipClientModule
files.Summary: Contract every SDK module implements to be discoverable through . Module packages define their own feature interfaces on top of this marker; the SDK core never references concrete module types.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Modules.ModuleFeatureUnavailableException
files.Summary: Thrown by when no registered module implements the requested feature contract.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.PolicyManager
files.Summary: Policy facade exposing active SDK policy defaults.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.QualityManager
files.Summary: Runtime quality facade for call-level snapshots and subscriptions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.SessionManager
files.Summary: Read-only runtime view over active calls and media sessions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.SipTransport
files.Summary: Default SIP signaling transport a consumer selects via . Determines which transport the SDK uses for outbound requests and its advertised local contact when a target URI does not force a transport (for example via a sips: scheme or ;transport= parameter). All five transports are listened on regardless; this only selects the default for outbound routing.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.TelemetryManager
files.Summary: Telemetry facade for SIP events, metrics, and call-detail records.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.VoipClient
files.Summary: Public SDK facade composing line management, call management, and media routing. This is the primary entry point for SDK consumers.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.VoipClientInitializationException
files.Summary: Signals that the VoIP SDK client could not be initialized due to runtime environment constraints.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.VoipConfiguration
files.Summary: Top-level SDK configuration: identity, transport security, logging, media/codec behavior, and call-lifecycle timeouts. Supplied once when constructing or registering the SDK.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.VoipOptions
files.Summary: Options model used by AddCalloraVoip(...) for host-based configuration.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.DtmfTone
files.Summary: One fully received inbound DTMF tone (RFC 4733 telephone-event), surfaced on once per tone after its end-of-event packet.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.EncodedFrame
files.Summary: One encoded media frame received on a . Transport-only: is the raw depacketised codec bitstream — the app owns decoding.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IEncodedMediaSink
files.Summary: A destination for recorded encoded media. The recorder writes every captured here (both directions, audio and video); the sink decides how to persist it — a per-track file, a container, an upload, an in-memory buffer, … Transport-only: payloads are the raw codec bitstream (no decoding). A container writer (Opus→Ogg, VP8→IVF, muxed MP4/…) is a concrete sink implementation.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IMediaTap
files.Summary: An L3 observer of the encoded media flowing through an in both directions — the extension seam for recording, analytics, or feeding an AI pipeline without owning the peer. Attach with and dispose the handle to detach.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IPeerConnection
files.Summary: A signalling-neutral WebRTC peer connection: the app owns the signalling channel (WebSocket/HTTP/ Callora) and exchanges SDP through it, while this peer runs ICE, DTLS-SRTP, BUNDLE and the RTP/RTCP transport. Encoded media flows through / — the SDK is transport-only, so the app owns the codec. The central per-connection abstraction of the WebRTC facade, mirroring the SIP ICall.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IPeerConnectionManager
files.Summary: The L2 registry of peer connections opened through a — the WebRTC counterpart to the SIP ICallManager. Peers are tracked on creation and removed when disposed, so a multi-peer app (e.g. a conference) can enumerate its live connections without keeping its own list.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcClient
files.Summary: The WebRTC peer facade — the happy-path entry point for building signalling-neutral peer connections. Mirrors the SIP IVoipClient; the advanced peer-registry manager tier is added in a later slice (ADR-012).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcClientModule
files.Summary: Contract every WebRTC facade module implements to be discoverable through — the L3 plugin seam, mirroring the SIP IVoipClientModule. Module packages define their own feature interfaces on top of this marker; the facade never references concrete module types.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcModuleRegistry
files.Summary: Thread-safe registry resolving optional WebRTC facade modules by their feature contract.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcRecorder
files.Summary: Records the encoded media flowing through a peer connection into an — an L3 module built on . Transport-only: it captures the raw codec bitstream per track and direction; muxing to a playable container is the sink's job.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcRecording
files.Summary: A running recording of a peer connection. Stop it with (or by disposing) to detach from the peer and flush the sink.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcSignaling
files.Summary: The app-owned signalling channel the SDK drives during : a bidirectional transport (WebSocket, HTTP long-poll, Callora signalling, …) that carries SDP descriptions between the two peers. The SDK stays signalling-neutral — it only sends and receives SDP through this contract; the app decides how the bytes travel.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcTrickleSignaling
files.Summary: Extends with out-of-band ICE candidate trickle (RFC 8838): alongside the offer/answer, the SDK sends each locally gathered candidate as it is discovered and applies remote candidates as they arrive, rather than relying only on the candidates the SDP carried. When an app supplies this on , the SDK gathers server-reflexive candidates and runs the trickle exchange; a plain stays SDP-only. Candidate strings are RFC 8829 candidate:… lines — the wire form browsers exchange verbatim, so no conversion is needed at the app boundary.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.MediaDirection
files.Summary: The direction of media observed by an .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.PeerConnectionState
files.Summary: Lifecycle state of a WebRTC , modelled on the W3C RTCPeerConnectionState (RFC 8829): a signalling-neutral view of where the peer is between creation and teardown. ICE consent and the DTLS handshake drive the transitions.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.RecordedFrame
files.Summary: One encoded media frame captured for recording, tagged with its track kind and direction. Transport-only: is the raw depacketised codec bitstream (the recorder does not decode).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.RemoteTrack
files.Summary: A remote media track surfaced by (the W3C per-track model). Encoded frames arrive on .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.TrackKind
files.Summary: The media kind of a remote WebRTC track surfaced by .

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcClient
files.Summary: The WebRTC peer facade (Level 1). Builds signalling-neutral s that run ICE, DTLS-SRTP, BUNDLE and RTP/RTCP internally; the app owns signalling and the codec (transport only). Zero-config new WebRtcClient() offers Opus over an ephemeral loopback endpoint with a fresh per-peer DTLS identity. See ADR-012 for the four-level design and the manager/DI tiers to come.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcConfiguration
files.Summary: Immutable configuration for a (the direct-construction surface; the DI/options path adds a mutable counterpart in a later slice). All fields are optional — a zero-config new WebRtcClient() binds an ephemeral loopback endpoint, offers Opus audio, and uses a fresh per-peer DTLS identity.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcConnectException
files.Summary: Thrown by when the peer connection fails to establish — the connection reached or was closed during negotiation (ICE or DTLS-SRTP did not complete).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcMediaStreamStats
files.Summary: A per-media-stream quality snapshot within a peer connection's (the SDK's getStats per-stream breakdown). One entry per audio/video track (folded by MID), plus one per remote inbound source whose payload type could not be attributed to a track. The two directions carry different metrics — and describe our outbound stream (keyed per our sending SSRC, RFC 3550 §6.4.1), while is our local receive-side jitter for the remote inbound source (RFC 3550 §A.8). A metric reads null until it is available for that stream — never a fabricated zero.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcOptions
files.Summary: Host-facing, mutable options for the WebRTC facade, consumed by AddCalloraWebRtc(...) — the dependency-injection counterpart to the immutable (mirrors how VoipOptions backs VoipConfiguration for the SIP facade).

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcPeerConnectionExtensions
files.Summary: The signalling happy path (Level 1) for : given an app-owned channel, the SDK drives the full offer/answer handshake and returns once the connection is established — the WebRTC counterpart to the SIP DialAndWaitUntilConnectedAsync. The neutral primitives (, , ) remain for callers that drive signalling themselves.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcRecorder
files.Summary: The built-in WebRTC recorder (L3 module). Attaches a media tap to a peer and streams every encoded frame (audio and video, both directions) into an . Usable standalone (new WebRtcRecorder().Start(peer, sink)) or registered as an and resolved via client.Modules.Get<IWebRtcRecorder>(). Transport-only: it captures the raw codec bitstream — turning it into a playable file is the sink's job.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcRole
files.Summary: The negotiation role a peer takes in the SDK-driven handshake ().

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.WebRtcStats
files.Summary: A point-in-time statistics snapshot for a peer connection (the SDK's getStats). Transport counters, derived bitrates and the ICE state/selected pair are populated today; quality, video and congestion-control metrics are added in later slices and read null until then — a null value means "not yet measured", never a fabricated zero.
